input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS, from scratch.

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **What to install:** Latest stable Xcode (currently Xcode 16.x)
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select --switch /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Discover versions:** `xcodebuild -version` — check the [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for supported Xcode versions.

---

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) — get the latest LTS or STS that supports MAUI.
- Use the **macOS installer (`.pkg`)** for arm64 or x64 as appropriate.
- **Discover versions:** The MAUI docs specify the minimum .NET version (e.g., .NET 8 or .NET 9). Check [MAUI supported platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms).
- **Verify:** `dotnet --list-sdks`

---

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

- Install after the SDK:
  ```
  sudo dotnet workload install maui
  ```
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` (and sub-workloads like `maui-ios`, `maui-android`).

---

## 4. Android SDK, JDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload installs Android build tooling, but you need the Android SDK and a JDK:

- **Option A (Recommended):** Let the MAUI workload handle it:
  ```
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net8.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" \
    -p:JavaSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Java" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
  Run this inside a new MAUI project (see step 6).

- **Option B (Manual):**
  - **JDK:** Microsoft OpenJDK 17 from [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)
  - **Android SDK:** Install [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio), then via SDK Manager install:
    - Android SDK Platform (API level matching MAUI's target — check release notes, typically API 34/35)
    - Android SDK Build-Tools
    - Android Emulator + system image for testing
  - Set environment variables in `~/.zshrc`:
    ```bash
    export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
    export JAVA_HOME="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/microsoft-17.jdk/Contents/Home"
    export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/emulator:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH"
    ```

- **Discover required versions:** Run `dotnet workload search maui` and check [MAUI Android requirements](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/android/emulator/setup).
- **Verify:**
  ```
  adb --version
  java -version          # Should show OpenJDK 17
  emulator -list-avds    # Should list at least one AVD
  ```

---

## 5. iOS Simulator Setup

- Comes with Xcode. Open **Xcode → Settings → Platforms** and download any additional simulator runtimes you need (e.g., iOS 18.x).
- **Verify:** `xcrun simctl list devices available`

---

## 6. Create & Build a Test Project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=SIMULATOR_UDID

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if using .NET 9.

---

## 7. Optional but Recommended

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com/) | Lightweight IDE with MAUI support |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued) | N/A — use VS Code instead | — |
| **.NET MAUI Extension** for VS Code | VS Code Marketplace | Hot reload, XAML preview |

---

## Quick Verification Checklist

```bash
xcodebuild -version           # Xcode version
dotnet --list-sdks             # .NET SDK installed
dotnet workload list           # maui workload present
java -version                  # JDK 17
adb --version                  # Android platform-tools
xcrun simctl list devices      # iOS simulators available
dotnet new maui -n Test && cd Test && dotnet build -f net8.0-android  # Builds clean
```

If all seven checks pass, you're ready to develop.
